title: Diagnostic plots
text: Plots log2 fold-change average concentration. Points coloured significance status.
code: plot_md(   object     = example_peaks,   x_var      = "Conc",   y_var      = "Fold",   fdr_var    = "FDR",   cutoff_y   = 0.5,   cutoff_FDR = 0.05,   title      = "MD plot — degron vs notag" )

title: Diagnostic plots
text: Density histogram normalized binding counts faceted chromosome.
code: plot_distributions(   object    = example_peaks,   title     = "Binding count distributions",   condition = "Degron",   baseline  = "NoTag",   type      = "density" ) plot_distributions(   object    = example_peaks,   title     = "Binding count distributions",   condition = "Degron",   baseline  = "NoTag",   type      = "hist" )

title: Diagnostic plots
text: T-test comparing log2(fold-change) distribution ChrA vs ChrA ChrX vs ChrA.  underlying data also accessible:
code: res <- ttest_scatter(   object   = example_peaks,   title    = "FC t-test per chromosome",   fc_col   = "Fold" ) res$plot res$results #>   Condition      p_value chr_to_evaluate       comparison_type mean_target #> 1      chrI 1.888820e-01            chrI Autosome vs Autosomes  0.11456884 #> 2     chrII 1.860554e-01           chrII Autosome vs Autosomes  0.07455821 #> 3    chrIII 1.966083e-01          chrIII Autosome vs Autosomes  0.11629048 #> 4     chrIV 7.369995e-01           chrIV Autosome vs Autosomes  0.08977472 #> 5      chrV 4.148876e-01            chrV Autosome vs Autosomes  0.08367961 #> 6      chrX 1.963583e-91            chrX        X vs Autosomes  0.99258303 #>   mean_reference neg_log10_pvalue is_significant #> 1     0.09018873        0.7238094          FALSE #> 2     0.09996571        0.7303578          FALSE #> 3     0.09045058        0.7063983          FALSE #> 4     0.09634571        0.1325328          FALSE #> 5     0.09837447        0.3820696          FALSE #> 6     0.09501824       90.7069507           TRUE

title: Violin plots
text: violin functions compare binding distributions chromosomes. Four functions cover main use cases:

title: Violin plots
text: Compares chrX peaks autosomes pooled. Welch t-test p-value annotated automatically.
code: violin_log2FC(   object          = example_peaks,   title           = "log2FC: chrX vs autosomes",   ylab            = "log2(Fold Change)",   chr_of_interest = "chrX",   ylim            = c(-3, 3),   pvalue_y_position = 2.5 )

title: Violin plots
text: Shows four groups: baseline-, baseline-X, condition-, condition-X.
code: violin_counts(   object           = example_peaks,   title            = "Normalized counts: chrX vs autosomes",   conc_condition   = "Conc_degron",   condition_name   = "degron",   conc_baseline    = "Conc_notag",   baseline_name    = "notag",   ylim             = c(0, 16),   label.y_plot     = c(13, 14, 15, 16) ) #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ercantools package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/ercanlab/ercantools/issues>. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Violin plots
text: One violin per chromosome significance brackets vs chrX.
code: violin_log2FC_all_chr(   object          = example_peaks,   title           = "log2FC per chromosome",   ylab            = "log2(Fold Change)",   chr_of_interest = "chrX",   ylim            = c(-3, 3) )

title: Violin plots
text: Two paired violins per chromosome separated condition.
code: violin_counts_all_chr(   object           = example_peaks,   title            = "Normalized counts per chromosome",   conc_condition   = "Conc_degron",   condition_name   = "degron",   conc_baseline    = "Conc_notag",   baseline_name    = "notag",   ylim             = c(0, 16) )

what: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
title: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
text: ercantools provides suite plotting statistical analysis functions ChIP-seq differential binding data, designed work directly DiffBind output.

title: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
text: Violin plots: violin_log2FC(), violin_counts(), violin_log2FC_all_chr(), violin_counts_all_chr() T-test scatter plots: ttest_scatter() DiffBind diagnostic plots: plot_md(), plot_distributions() Peak utilities: find_overlap_peaks(), upset_peaks()

title: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
text: Functions designed C. elegans (chrI-V + chrX) default, chromosome parameters fully configurable organisms.

title: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
text: synthetic dataset mimicking DiffBind output included:
code: data(example_peaks)

title: ercantools: Visualization and Analysis Tools for ChIP-seq Differential Binding Data — ercantools-package
text: Maintainer: Daniel Garbozo dg4739@nyu.edu contributors: Sevinc Ercan sercan@nyu.edu [contributor]

what: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks
title: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks
text: synthetic dataset 2000 ChIP-seq peaks designed mimic output DiffBind::dba.report(dba, th = 1, bCounts = TRUE) C. elegans dosage compensation experiment. Peaks chrX simulated higher fold change degron condition, reflecting dosage compensation loss relevant factor degraded.

title: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks
code: example_peaks

title: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks
text: data.frame 2000 rows 11 columns: seqnames Character. Chromosome name (chrI-chrV, chrX). start Integer. Peak start position (bp). end Integer. Peak end position (bp). width Integer. Peak width bp (always 501). strand Character. Always "*" (unstranded ChIP-seq). Conc Numeric. Average normalized binding count across conditions. Conc_degron Numeric. Normalized counts degron condition. Conc_notag Numeric. Normalized counts -tag baseline. Fold Numeric. log2 fold change (degron / notag). p.value Numeric. Raw p-value DiffBind. FDR Numeric. Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value.

title: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks
text: Generated synthetically via data-raw/example_peaks.R. Proportions effect sizes based real C. elegans dosage compensation ChIP-seq experiment.

title: Example ChIP-seq differential binding peaks — example_peaks

what: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
title: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
text: Expands peak window mid ± bp_distance (mid = (start + end) / 2) uses GenomicRanges::findOverlaps() identify peaks set overlap . logical has_overlap_peak column appended data frames.

title: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
code: find_overlap_peaks(df1, df2, bp_distance)

title: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
text: df1 data.frame least seqnames, start, end columns. df2 data.frame requirements df1. bp_distance Numeric. Half-width base pairs window around peak midpoint used overlap detection.

title: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
text: named list three elements: df1: input df1 added has_overlap_peak logical column. df2: input df2 added has_overlap_peak logical column. df2_percentage: Character string reporting percentage df2 peaks overlap df1 (e.g., "72.34% (362/500)").

title: Find overlapping peaks between two peak sets — find_overlap_peaks
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_peaks) peaks_a <- example_peaks[example_peaks$seqnames == "chrX", ] peaks_b <- example_peaks[example_peaks$seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrI"), ]  res <- find_overlap_peaks(peaks_a, peaks_b, bp_distance = 100) cat(res$df2_percentage) head(res$df1) } # }

what: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
title: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
text: Plots distribution normalized binding counts two experimental conditions, faceted chromosome. Supports density histogram geometries, well overlaid grid facetting.

title: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
code: plot_distributions(   object,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   chromosomes = .elegans_chr_order(),   conc_cols = c(Conc_base = "Conc_notag", Conc_condition = "Conc_degron"),   condition = "Degron",   baseline = "NoTag",   type = c("density", "hist"),   overlay = TRUE,   ncol = 3,   free_y = TRUE,   add_rug = FALSE )

title: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
text: object data.frame tibble. Must contain seqnames column. title Character. Plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default NULL. chromosomes Character vector. Chromosomes include. Default C. elegans order. conc_cols Named character vector mapping role column name. Default c(Conc_base = "Conc_notag", Conc_condition = "Conc_degron"). condition Character. Display name condition. Default "Degron". baseline Character. Display name baseline. Default "NoTag". type Character. "density" (default) "hist". overlay Logical. TRUE (default), conditions overlaid within chromosome facet. FALSE, Condition ~ seqnames grid used. ncol Integer. Number facet columns. Default 3. free_y Logical. TRUE (default), facet y-axes free. add_rug Logical. Add rug x-axis? Default FALSE.

title: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Density or histogram of normalized binding counts per chromosome — plot_distributions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ plot_distributions(   object    = my_peaks,   title     = "Binding count distributions",   condition = "Degron",   baseline  = "NoTag" ) } # }

what: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
title: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
text: Plots log2 fold-change (y-axis) average abundance/normalized binding counts (x-axis), points coloured significance status. Optionally labels user-provided set peaks ggrepel::geom_label_repel().

title: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
code: plot_md(   object,   x_var = "Conc",   y_var = "Fold",   fdr_var = "FDR",   cutoff_y = 1,   cutoff_FDR = 0.05,   y_max = 3,   y_min = -3,   label_var = NULL,   peaks = NULL,   title = "MD Plot",   colors = c("red", "grey70", "blue") )

title: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
text: object data.frame tibble — typically output DiffBind::dba.report() coerced data frame. x_var Character. Column average abundance(normalized binding counts). Default "Conc". y_var Character. Column log2 fold-change. Default "Fold". fdr_var Character. Column FDR values. Default "FDR". cutoff_y Numeric. log2FC threshold coloring. Default 1. cutoff_FDR Numeric. FDR threshold coloring. Default 0.05. y_max Numeric. Upper y-axis limit. Default 3. y_min Numeric. Lower y-axis limit. Default -3. label_var Character. Column used peak labels. Default NULL. peaks Character vector. Peak IDs label. Default NULL. title Character. Plot title. Default "MD Plot". colors Character vector length 3. Colors -regulated, -change, -regulated points. Default c("red", "grey70", "blue").

title: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: MA / MD plot for differential binding results — plot_md
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ plot_md(   object  = my_peaks,   x_var   = "Conc",   y_var   = "Fold",   fdr_var = "FDR",   title   = "MD plot — degron vs notag" ) } # }

what: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
title: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
text: chromosome, runs Welch t-test log2 fold-change values ("Autosome vs Autosomes" "X vs Autosomes") displays results dot plot.

title: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
code: ttest_scatter(   object = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   ylab = "-log10(p-value)",   fc_col = "Fold",   chr_col = "seqnames",   autosomes = c("chrI", "chrII", "chrIII", "chrIV", "chrV"),   chr_X = "chrX",   chr_colors = c(chrI = "#66C2A5", chrII = "#FC8D62", chrIII = "#8DA0CB", chrIV =     "#E78AC3", chrV = "#A6D854", chrX = "#EF9A9A"),   pvalue_threshold_y = -log10(0.05),   pvalue_y_position_offset = 0.5,   pvalue_x_position = 6,   output_pdf = NULL,   pdf_width = 8,   pdf_height = 6 )

title: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
text: object data.frame tibble. title Character. Main plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default NULL. ylab Character. Y-axis label. Default "-log10(p-value)". fc_col Character. Column name log2 fold-change. Default "Fold". chr_col Character. Chromosome column name. Default "seqnames". autosomes Character vector. Autosome names. chr_X Character. Sex chromosome name. Default "chrX". chr_colors Named character vector. Colors per chromosome. pvalue_threshold_y Numeric. Significance threshold -log10 scale. Default -log10(0.05). pvalue_y_position_offset Numeric. Vertical offset (plot units) placing p-value threshold label horizontal reference line.  Default: 0.5. pvalue_x_position Numeric. Absolute X position (plot units) p-value threshold label, right plot. Default 6. output_pdf Character. Optional PDF output path. Default NULL. pdf_width Numeric. Default 8. pdf_height Numeric. Default 6.

title: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
text: named list plot results.

title: Dot plot of -log10(p-value) for log2 fold-change comparisons along chromosomes — ttest_scatter
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- ttest_scatter(   object = my_peaks,   title  = "FC t-test (Autosome vs Autosomes and X vs Autosomes)" ) res$plot } # }

what: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
title: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
text: Creates UpSetR::upset() plot peak data frame containing logical has_overlap_* columns produced repeated calls find_overlap_peaks(). column matching has_overlap_ automatically detected sets NULL.

title: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
code: upset_peaks(   object,   sets = NULL,   set_labels = NULL,   order_by = "freq",   set_colors = c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00", "#A65628"),   title = NULL,   output_pdf = NULL,   width = 12,   height = 8 )

title: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
text: object data.frame logical columns named has_overlap_*. sets Character vector. Column names include. NULL (default), has_overlap_* columns used automatically. set_labels Character vector. Display labels set. NULL, labels derived removing has_overlap_ prefix. order_by Character. "freq" (default) "degree". set_colors Character vector. Bar colours set. title Character. Optional title plot. Default NULL. output_pdf Character. Optional path save PDF. Default NULL. width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 12. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 8.

title: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
text: Invisibly returns UpSet plot object.

title: UpSet plot for peak category overlaps — upset_peaks
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(example_peaks) example_peaks$has_overlap_promoter <- sample(c(TRUE, FALSE),                                              nrow(example_peaks),                                              replace = TRUE) example_peaks$has_overlap_enhancer <- sample(c(TRUE, FALSE),                                              nrow(example_peaks),                                              replace = TRUE) upset_peaks(example_peaks, title = "Peak category overlaps") } # }

what: Creates violin-box plots to compare normalized binding counts values between different conditions and chromosomes, typically comparing X chromosome vs autosomes. — violin_counts

title: Creates violin-box plots to compare normalized binding counts values between different conditions and chromosomes, typically comparing X chromosome vs autosomes. — violin_counts

text: object data.frame tibble. title Character. Main plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default NULL. ylab Character. Y-axis label. Default log2(Normalized binding counts). xlab Character. X-axis label. Default NULL. conc_condition Character. Column name condition counts. Default "Conc_degron". condition_name Character. Display name condition. Default "degron". conc_baseline Character. Column name baseline counts. Default "Conc_notag". baseline_name Character. Display name baseline. Default "notag". chr_col Character. Chromosome column name. Default "seqnames". chr_of_interest Character. Chromosome highlight. Default "chrX". ylim Numeric vector length 2. Default c(0, 12). chromosomes Character vector. Chromosomes included order. color_condition Character. Fill colour condition. Default "#ff6347ff". color_baseline Character. Fill colour baseline. Default "#4682b4ff". output_pdf Character. Optional path save plot PDF. Default NULL. pdf_width Numeric. Default 14. pdf_height Numeric. Default 7.

what: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC
title: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC
text: function generate violin-box plot compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) chromosome X autosomes () based ChIPseq data also performs t-test assess statistical significance.

title: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC
code: violin_log2FC(   object = NULL,   title = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   log2FC_col = "Fold",   chr_col = "seqnames",   chr_of_interest = "chrX",   color_chr_of_interest = "#EF9A9A",   color_other_chr = "#4DB6AC",   ylim = c(-1, 1),   pvalue_y_position = 0.9 )

title: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC
text: object Data frame containing chromosomal fold change data (diffbind report output) title Plot title (optional) ylab Y-axis label (optional) xlab X-axis label (optional) log2FC_col Column name containing log2FoldChange values (default: "Fold") chr_col Column name containing chromosome identifiers (default: "seqnames") chr_of_interest Chromosome interest highlight (default: "chrX") color_chr_of_interest Color chromosome interest (default: "#EF9A9A") color_other_chr Color chromosomes (default: "#4DB6AC") ylim Y-axis limits (default: c(-1,1)) pvalue_y_position Vertical position p-value text (default: 0.9)

title: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC
text: ggplot object containing violin box plots

title: A function to generate violin-box plot to compare binding changes (log2FoldChange) between chromosome X and autosomes (A) based on ChIPseq data It also performs a t-test to assess statistical significance. — violin_log2FC

what: Violin + box plot for all chromosomes separately (log2 fold change) — violin_log2FC_all_chr
title: Violin + box plot for all chromosomes separately (log2 fold change) — violin_log2FC_all_chr
text: Draws one violin + boxplot per chromosome compares autosome chromosome interest using ggpubr::stat_compare_means().

title: Violin + box plot for all chromosomes separately (log2 fold change) — violin_log2FC_all_chr
code: violin_log2FC_all_chr(   object = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   ylab = "log2 Fold Change",   xlab = NULL,   log2FC_col = "Fold",   chr_col = "seqnames",   chr_of_interest = "chrX",   color_chr_of_interest = "#EF9A9A",   color_other_chr = c("#4DB6AC", "#4DB6AC", "#4DB6AC", "#4DB6AC", "#4DB6AC"),   ylim = c(-2, 2),   chromosomes = .elegans_chr_order(),   output_pdf = NULL,   pdf_width = 12,   pdf_height = 6 )

title: Violin + box plot for all chromosomes separately (log2 fold change) — violin_log2FC_all_chr
text: object data.frame tibble DiffBind::dba.report(). title Character. Main plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default NULL. ylab Character. Y-axis label. Default log2 Fold Change. xlab Character. X-axis label. Default NULL. log2FC_col Character. Column name log2 fold-change. Default "Fold". chr_col Character. Column name chromosome identifiers. Default "seqnames". chr_of_interest Character. Chromosome highlight. Default "chrX". color_chr_of_interest Character. Fill colour chrX. Default "#EF9A9A". color_other_chr Character. Fill colour autosomes. Default "#4DB6AC". ylim Numeric vector length 2. Y-axis limits. Default c(-2, 2). chromosomes Character vector. Chromosomes include order. Default C. elegans order: c("chrI","chrII","chrIII","chrIV","chrV","chrX"). output_pdf Character. Optional path save plot PDF. Default NULL. pdf_width Numeric. PDF width inches. Default 12. pdf_height Numeric. PDF height inches. Default 6.

title: Violin + box plot for all chromosomes separately (log2 fold change) — violin_log2FC_all_chr
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.
